channel.title: Azure News Feed
channel.description: Aggregated daily news from Azure blogs

item.title: Build Your AI Agent in 5 Minutes with AI Toolkit for VS Code
item.description: What if building an AI agent was as easy as filling out a form? No frameworks to install. No boilerplate to copy-paste from GitHub. No YAML to debug at midnight. Just VS Code, one extension, and an idea. AI Toolkit for VS Code turns agent development into something anyone can do — whether you're a...
item.dc:creator: isha_sahni

item.title: Using the Microsoft 365 Connector for Claude
item.description: The Microsoft 365 Connector for Claude allows Claude to access SharePoint and OneDrive files, emails, and Teams chats and meetings. The connector is now available to all users, including the free tier for Claude. Installing the connector creates two Entra ID enterprise apps (MCP server and client)...
item.dc:creator: TonyRedmond

item.title: Monthly news - April 2026
item.description: Microsoft DefenderMonthly news - April 2026 Edition This is our monthly "What's new" blog post, summarizing product updates and various new assets we released over the past month across our Defender products. In this edition, we are looking at all the goodness from March 2026. We are now including...
item.dc:creator: HeikeRitter

item.title: How to clear cache on iphone to free up more space
item.description: I have an iPhone 13 that I've been using for a couple of years, and I'm down to just 5GB of free space. I already deleted unused apps and old photos, but storage is still tight. I know Android phones have a straightforward way to clear app cache, but on iOS, it seems less obvious.How do I...
item.dc:creator: TheoDalton

item.title: GitHub Copilot for Eclipse Is Going Open Source
item.description: We’re excited to announce that the GitHub Copilot for Eclipse plugin is going open source under the MIT license and will be hosted on GitHub under the microsoft organization in the coming weeks. By open-sourcing the project, we’re inviting the community to contribute, extend, and innovate, helping...
item.dc:creator: Jialuo Gan

item.title: AZD for Beginners: A Practical Introduction to Azure Developer CLI
item.description: If you are learning how to get an application from your machine into Azure without stitching together every deployment step by hand, Azure Developer CLI, usually shortened to azd, is one of the most useful tools to understand early. It gives developers a workflow-focused command line for...
item.dc:creator: Lee_Stott

item.title: Simplify Azure Arc Server Onboarding with Ansible and the New Onboarding Role
item.description: If you’re already using Ansible to manage your infrastructure, there’s now a simpler—and more secure—way to bring machines under Azure Arc management. We’ve introduced a new Azure Arc onboarding role designed specifically for automated scenarios like Ansible playbooks. This role follows the...
item.dc:creator: alinetran

item.title: Azure Container Storage v2.1.0: Now GA with Elastic SAN
item.description: Explore what’s new in Azure Container Storage v2.1.0 with Elastic SAN integration, modular on-demand installation, and enhanced node selector support.
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: 3 Ways to Get More from Azure SRE Agent
item.description: When you first set up Azure SRE Agent, it’s tempting to give it everything. Connect all your alert sources, route every severity, set up scheduled tasks to poll your channels every 30 seconds. The agent can handle all of it. But a few simple configuration choices can help you get more value from...
item.dc:creator: dchelupati

item.title: Azure Monitor in Azure SRE Agent: Autonomous Alert Investigation and Intelligent Merging
item.description: Azure Monitor is great at telling you something is wrong. But once the alert fires, the real work begins — someone has to open the portal, triage it, dig into logs, and figure out what happened. That takes time. And while they're investigating, the same alert keeps firing every few minutes,...
item.dc:creator: Vineela-Suri

item.title: MCP as Easy as 1-2-3: Introducing the Fluent API for MCP Apps
item.description: Build MCP tools with rich UI experiences using the new Fluent API for MCP Apps in the .NET isolated worker. Configure views, permissions, and security policies with just a few lines of code. The post MCP as Easy as 1-2-3: Introducing the Fluent API for MCP Apps appeared first on Azure SDK Blog.
item.dc:creator: Lilian Kasem (she/her)

item.title: MCP Apps now available in Copilot chat
item.description: Agents bring rich, app powered UI experiences directly into Microsoft 365 Copilot chat, via MCP Apps or the OpenAI Apps SDK. Read about what customers and partners are building. The post MCP Apps now available in Copilot chat appeared first on Microsoft 365 Developer Blog.
item.dc:creator: Deepak Pratinidhi

item.title: OneDrive Photos Restyle with AI-now rolling out on mobile and web
item.description: Photos capture real moments. With AI Restyle in OneDrive, you can reimagine them in fresh new styles-right where your photos already live. Meet AI Restyle Photos capture real moments. With AI Restyle in OneDrive, you can reimagine those moments in expressive new styles-right where your photos...
item.dc:creator: Shailesh_kv

item.title: RSAC 2026: New Microsoft Sentinel Connectors Announcement
item.description: Microsoft Sentinel helps organizations detect, investigate, and respond to security threats across increasingly complex environments. With the rollout of the Microsoft Sentinel data lake in the fall, and the App Assure-backed Sentinel promise that supports it, customers now have access to...
item.dc:creator: JesseKopavi

item.title: Your Migration’s Source of Truth: The Modernization Assessment
item.description: Introducing GitHub Copilot Modernization and diving into the assessment report as part of cloud modernization process The post Your Migration’s Source of Truth: The Modernization Assessment appeared first on .NET Blog.
item.dc:creator: Jeffrey Fritz

item.title: Agentic development aspirations: build, run, observe — without more Markdown
item.description: AI agents can write code but struggle to run and test it. Aspire gives your agent a codified app model to build, run, and observe your whole stack. The post Agentic development aspirations: build, run, observe — without more Markdown appeared first on Aspire Blog.
item.dc:creator: Maddy Montaquila

item.title: ASP.NET Core 2.3 end of support announcement
item.description: ASP.NET Core 2.3 will reach end of support on April 7, 2027. Learn what this means for your applications and how to upgrade to a modern supported version of .NET. The post ASP.NET Core 2.3 end of support announcement appeared first on .NET Blog.
item.dc:creator: Daniel Roth

item.title: Logic Apps Aviators Newsletter - April 2026
item.description: In this issue: Ace Aviator of the Month News from our product group News from our community Ace Aviator of the Month April 2026's Ace Aviator: Marcelo Gomes Marcelo Gomes - Integration Team Leader @ COFCO International What’s your role and title? What are your responsibilities? I’m an Integration...
item.dc:creator: WSilveira

item.title: Scalable AI with Azure Cosmos DB: Tredence Intelligent Document Processing (IDP) | March 2026
item.description: Azure Cosmos DB enables scalable AI-driven document processing, addressing one of the biggest barriers to operational scale in today’s enterprise AI landscape. Organizations continue to manage massive volumes of structured and unstructured content—contracts, regulatory filings, operational records,...
item.dc:creator: Manish Sharma

item.title: Unily app within Teams
item.description: we are in the process of migrating from our current intranet vendor to Unily, and we had full Teams integration as a requirement during the RFP process. we were told it existed. fast forward to last week when we asked about it and we were told there is no Unily app for Teams. i could've sworn this...
item.dc:creator: kbowling580

item.title: Write SQL Your Way: Dual Parameter Style Benefits in mssql-python
item.description: Reviewed by: Sumit Sarabhai If you’ve been writing SQL in Python, you already know the debate: positional parameters (?) or named parameters (%(name)s)? Some developers swear by the conciseness of positional. Others prefer the clarity of named. With mssql-python, you no longer need to choose – we...
item.dc:creator: Jahnvi Thakkar

item.title: Prioritize security alerts with runtime context from Dynatrace
item.description: You can now use runtime context from Dynatrace to prioritize GitHub Advanced Security alerts based on deployed artifacts and runtime risk in your Kubernetes environment. When you connect Dynatrace to… The post Prioritize security alerts with runtime context from Dynatrace appeared first on The...
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Copilot CLI now supports BYOK and local models
item.description: GitHub Copilot CLI now lets you connect your own model provider or run fully local models instead of using GitHub-hosted model routing. This means you can use the models and… The post Copilot CLI now supports BYOK and local models appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Dependabot alerts are now assignable to AI agents for remediation
item.description: Some dependency vulnerabilities require more than a version bump—they need code changes across your project. You can now assign Dependabot alerts to AI coding agents, including Copilot, Claude, and Codex,… The post Dependabot alerts are now assignable to AI agents for remediation appeared first on...
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Dependabot version updates now support the Nix ecosystem
item.description: Dependabot now supports Nix flakes. Add nix as a package ecosystem in your dependabot.yml file. Dependabot will then monitor your flake.lock inputs and open pull requests when newer commits are… The post Dependabot version updates now support the Nix ecosystem appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Take full control of your floating windows in Visual Studio
item.description: Make Visual Studio floating windows work perfectly with PowerToys FancyZones. Flip one option to get independent windows, better snapping, and less friction. The post Take full control of your floating windows in Visual Studio appeared first on Visual Studio Blog.
item.dc:creator: Mads Kristensen

item.title: SOHO router compromise leads to DNS hijacking and adversary-in-the-middle attacks
item.description: Executive summary Forest Blizzard, a threat actor linked to the Russian military, has been compromising insecure home and small-office internet equipment like routers, then modifying their settings in ways that turn them into part of the actor’s malicious infrastructure. The post SOHO router...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft Threat Intelligence

item.title: Code scanning: Batch apply security alert suggestions on pull requests
item.description: GitHub code scanning alerts on pull requests are now easier to address with bulk actions. You can now apply fixes for code scanning alerts in the Files changed tab by… The post Code scanning: Batch apply security alert suggestions on pull requests appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Shortcut transformations: Turn files into Delta tables without pipelines (Generally Available)
item.description: Organizations today manage data across multiple storage systems, often in formats like CSV, Parquet, and JSON. While this data is readily available, turning it into analytics-ready tables typically requires building and maintaining complex ETL pipelines. Shortcut transformations remove that...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Secure data streaming: Custom CA and mTLS in Fabric Eventstream connectors (Preview)
item.description: Security is non-negotiable when it comes to real-time data streaming. In regulated industries such as banking, healthcare, and telecommunications, organizations must ensure that every data connection is encrypted and mutually authenticated. But many enterprises rely on internal Certificate...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: How do I completely uninstall applications on a mac?
item.description: Hi everyone! I'm a total newbie to the mac ecosystem, and I'm a bit confused about the proper way to remove software. Coming from Windows 10, I'm used to a dedicated uninstaller app, but on my Mac, I've mostly just been dragging app icons from the Applications folder into the Trash. I'm worried...
item.dc:creator: Stonehu

item.title: What is the best font changer for generating fancy fonts?
item.description: Look, I'm tired of plain text. I want those fancy Unicode fonts, the gothic, bubble, double-struck stuff but every font changer site I find is either broken or crawling with ads. What's the actual best font changer that gives you tons of styles and doesn't mess up when you copy-paste?Just need...
item.dc:creator: KellanWarner

item.title: iPhoneからAndroidにデータ移行はできますか？
item.description: 最近、新しいAndroidスマホに乗り換えようと思っているのですが、iPhoneからのデータ移行がうまくできるか不安です。写真や連絡先、メッセージなどをできるだけ簡単に移したいです。iphone android データ 移行のおすすめ方法があれば教えてください。特に、無料で安全にできる方法や、初心者でも失敗しにくいやり方を知りたいです。iphone android データ 移行を実際にやったことがある方、使ったツールや手順なども含めてアドバイスいただけると助かります。
item.dc:creator: Thatcherw

item.title: Discover and assess File Shares for migration to Azure Files with Azure Migrate
item.description: When organizations migrate to the cloud, applications are at the center of every decision. And behind every application is data, stored in file shares, accessed by users and services, and critical to keeping the application running. Moving that data seamlessly is not optional; it is a fundamental...
item.dc:creator: ankitsurkar

item.title: Why Data Platforms Must Become Intelligence Platforms for AI Agents to Work
item.description: The promise and the gap Your organization has invested in an AI agent. You ask it: "Prepare a summary of Q3 revenue by region, including year-over-year trends and top product lines." The agent finds revenue numbers in a SQL warehouse, product metadata in Dataverse, regional mappings in SharePoint,...
item.dc:creator: AnjaliSadhukhan

item.title: Webinar Cancellation
item.description: Hi everyone, The webinar “Re‑Envisioned: The New Single Device Experience in the Intune Admin Console,” originally scheduled for April 7 at 9:00 AM Pacific Time, has been cancelled at this time. We plan to reschedule the session, and when a new date is confirmed, it will be shared at...
item.dc:creator: emilyfalla

item.title: npm trusted publishing now supports CircleCI
item.description: npm trusted publishing now supports CircleCI as an OIDC provider, joining GitHub Actions and GitLab CI/CD. Maintainers publishing from CircleCI workflows can now eliminate stored credentials entirely and authenticate directly… The post npm trusted publishing now supports CircleCI appeared first on...
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: AI Inference on AKS enabled by Azure Arc: Predictive AI using Triton and ResNet-50
item.description: Run predictive AI at the edge by deploying Triton with the ONNX backend on AKS enabled by Azure Arc for local ResNet 50 GPU inference without cloud dependency.
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: AI Inference on AKS enabled by Azure Arc: Generative AI with Open‑Source LLM Server
item.description: Run generative AI at the edge with Ollama and vLLM on AKS enabled by Azure Arc, enabling local LLM inference on GPU hardware without dependency or data egress.
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: AI Inference on AKS enabled by Azure Arc: Series Introduction and Scope
item.description: Scenario driven series for generative and predictive AI inference on AKS enabled by Azure Arc, across CPUs, GPUs, and NPUs in on-premises and edge environments.
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: AI Inference on AKS enabled by Azure Arc: Bringing AI to the Edge and On‑Premises
item.description: Learn how to bring AI inference closer to your data with AKS enabled by Azure Arc, with practical hybrid and edge scenarios for secure, low-latency workloads.
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Agentic IIS Migration to Managed Instance on Azure App Service
item.description: Introduction Enterprises running ASP.NET Framework workloads on Windows Server with IIS face a familiar dilemma: modernize or stay put. The applications work, the infrastructure is stable, and nobody wants to be the person who breaks production during a cloud migration. But the cost of maintaining...
item.dc:creator: Gaurav-Seth

item.title: Copilot usage metrics now identify active and passive Copilot code review users
item.description: Copilot usage metrics now indicate which users have Copilot code review (CCR) activity, and whether that activity was active or passive. Enterprise and organization admins can see how users engage… The post Copilot usage metrics now identify active and passive Copilot code review users appeared...
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Introducing the New Microsoft Sentinel Logstash Output Plugin (Public Preview!)
item.description: Many organizations rely on Logstash as a flexible, trusted data pipeline for collecting, transforming, and forwarding logs from on-premises and hybrid environments. Microsoft Sentinel has long supported a Logstash output plugin, enabling customers to send data directly into Sentinel as part of...
item.dc:creator: JamesAde

item.title: Company Portal Profile installation failed on iPhone - Status code 400
item.description: Hello, I've been managing mobile devices through InTune for almost a year. Most of our devices are iOs - I add the phone to the Apple Business Manager - wait for it to appear in InTune - then install company portal, and log my user in. This pushed out software etc to the phone.I successfully set...
item.dc:creator: AmberH675

item.title: GitHub Copilot CLI combines model families for a second opinion
item.description: Discover how Rubber Duck provides a different perspective to GitHub Copilot CLI. The post GitHub Copilot CLI combines model families for a second opinion appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Nick McKenna

item.title: Announcing the end of support for Node.js 20.x in the Azure SDK for JavaScript
item.description: After July 9, 2026, the Azure SDK for JavaScript will no longer support Node.js 20.x. Upgrade to an Active Node.js Long Term Support (LTS) version to stay secure and up-to-date. The post Announcing the end of support for Node.js 20.x in the Azure SDK for JavaScript appeared first on Azure SDK Blog.
item.dc:creator: Minh-Anh Phan

item.title: Announcing Public Preview for Essential Machine Management
item.description: Managing servers and VMs across Azure, on premises, and multi-cloud environments often means turning on core capabilities—monitoring, updates, inventory, and configuration—through separate setup experiences. We’ve heard feedback that this makes it harder to get visibility into machine state and...
item.dc:creator: Meagan McCrory

item.title: Inside an AI‑enabled device code phishing campaign
item.description: A new wave of device code phishing shows how threat actors are scaling account compromise using AI and end‑to‑end automation. This campaign goes beyond traditional phishing by generating live authentication codes on demand, enabling higher success rates and sustained post‑compromise access. The...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft Defender Security Research Team

item.title: Storm-1175 focuses gaze on vulnerable web-facing assets in high-tempo Medusa ransomware operations
item.description: The financially motivated cybercriminal threat actor Storm-1175 operates high-velocity ransomware campaigns that weaponize recently disclosed vulnerabilities to obtain initial access, exfiltrate data, and deploy Medusa ransomware. The post Storm-1175 focuses gaze on vulnerable web-facing assets in...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft Threat Intelligence
